characteristic_fr: Le cap Leschenault marque le dĂŠbut de la plage de Seabird sur son cĂ´tĂŠ nord, dâoĂš le rivage sâoriente sur environ deux kilomĂ¨tres vers le nord-ouest en direction de Seabird, avant dâinflĂŠchir plus nettement vers le nord. En raison, sans doute, des processus dâensablement du littoral, plusieurs repĂ¨res observĂŠs par Hamelin sont aujourdâhui difficiles Ă  reconnaĂŽtre, notamment le cap Leschenault lui-mĂŞme, nommĂŠ en lâhonneur du botaniste de lâexpĂŠdition (voir ĂŠgalement lâentrĂŠe port Leschenault en Australie-Occidentale).
characteristic: Cape Leschenault marks the beginning of Seabird Beach on its northern flank, from which the shoreline trends approximately two kilometres to the north-west toward Seabird, before turning more distinctly northward. Owing, possibly, to coastal silting processes, several landmarks recorded by Hamelin are difficult to identify today, including Cape Leschenault itself, named in honour of the expeditionâs botanist (see also the entry Port Leschenault in Western Australia).
history_fr: $Hamelin$Jacques_FĂŠlix_Emmanuel_Hamelin$ signala une sĂŠrie de rĂŠcifs blancs sâĂŠtendant sur environ 200 kilomĂ¨tres le long de la cĂ´te. Lâun dâentre eux est aujourdâhui connu sous le nom de rĂŠcif de Leschenault, situĂŠ Ă  quelques kilomĂ¨tres au large du cap Leschenault. Voir aussi Port Leschenault en Australie occidentale.
history: Hamelin recorded a series of white reefs extending for approximately 200 kilometres along the coast. One of these is now known as Leschenault Reef, located a few kilometres offshore from Cape Leschenault. See also Port Leschenault in WA.

characteristic_fr:
Baie Breton, qui ne rĂŠpond plus â ou nâa jamais rĂŠpondu â Ă  la dĂŠfinition gĂŠographique dâune baie, est situĂŠe le long du littoral entre Seabird et Ledge Point, dans le comtĂŠ de Gingin (Shire of Gingin). 
Elle tire son nom de lâaspirant de marine Joseph DĂŠsirĂŠ Breton (1781â1816).
characteristic:
Breton Bay, which does not/no longer meets the geographical definition of a bay,  is located along the coast between Seabird and Ledge Point in the Shire of Gingin. 
It was named for the midshipman Joseph DĂŠsirĂŠ Breton (1781-1816).
history_fr: En 1801, Breton fut transfĂŠrĂŠ par Baudin du GĂŠographe au Naturaliste. Commentant avec colĂ¨re dans son journal de bord son transfert au Naturaliste, Breton compara le commandant Ă  un ÂŤ Tartuffe Âť [personnage de MoliĂ¨re particuliĂ¨rement faux, dĂŠvot et hypocrite] qui pleure et fait pleurer sur son malheur, mais agit avec hypocrisie et mĂŠchancetĂŠ. Il ecrit: "Le 7 [brumaire, 29 octobre 1801], le comd.t me donna l'ordre depasser Ă  bord du Naturaliste : comme cen'etait pas un ordre, mais un bien une lettre remplie de sottises & d'absurditĂŠs : je lâalui reportai. Nous eumes une trĂ¨s longue explication, ou le Tartuffe, me dit que c'ĂŠtait pour mon bien qu'il me mettait Ă  bord du Naturaliste et qu'il ne m'avait pas donnĂŠ d'avancement : il joua si bien son rĂ´le, qu'il me fit rĂŠpandre des pleurs, (& il en repandit aussi), et me fit croire, qu'il m'etait attachĂŠ, et que j'avais eu des torts avec lui : mais Ă  peine, l'eus-je quittĂŠ, qu'ayant rĂŠflechi, sur lamaniere dont il s'etait comportĂŠ envers moi & bien d'autres, depuis le commencement de la campagne, que jevis son hypocrisie & sa mĂŠchancetĂŠ ."
history: In 1801, Breton was transferred by Baudin from Le GĂŠographe to le Naturaliste. Commenting angrily in his sea journal on his transfer to le Naturaliste, Breton compared the commander to a âTartuffeâ [a character of MoliĂ¨re particularly false devout and hypocritical] who cries and makes cry of misfortune but acts with hypocrisy and wickedness.

characteristic_fr:
LâĂŽle Lancelin, situĂŠe Ă  proximitĂŠ de la localitĂŠ de Lancelin en Australie-Occidentale, est une rĂŠserve naturelle calcaire abritant dâimportantes colonies dâoiseaux marins ainsi que des populations de lions de mer. 
Cette petite ĂŽle doit son nom Ă  Pierre FranĂ§ois Lancelin (1768â1808), ingĂŠnieur constructeur de la Marine franĂ§aise, ĂŠgalement connu pour ses travaux philosophiques.
characteristic:
Lancelin Island, located near the Western Australian town of Lancelin, is a limestone nature reserve known for its large seabird colonies and populations of sea lions. 
The small island was named after Pierre FranĂ§ois Lancelin (1768â1808), a construction engineer with the French Navy who was also active as a philosopher.
history_fr:
Lorsque la classe des sciences morales et politiques de l' $Institut$Institut_de_France$ sâinterrogea sur le lien entre les signes â entendus au sens sĂŠmiotique, comme des symboles porteurs des concepts propres Ă  une culture â et la pensĂŠe, elle proposa un prix sur le thĂ¨me : ÂŤ DĂŠterminer lâinfluence des signes sur la formation des idĂŠes Âť. Ă cette occasion, Lancelin soumit un mĂŠmoire qui reĂ§ut une mention honorable. Le prix fut attribuĂŠ Ă  DegĂŠrando (voir lâĂŽle DegĂŠrando en Australie-Occidentale et le cap DegĂŠrando en Tasmanie).
Lancelin dĂŠveloppa par la suite ce travail et le publia entre 1801 et 1803 en trois volumes sous le titre Introduction Ă  lâanalyse des sciences, ou de la gĂŠnĂŠration, des fondemens et des instruments de nos connoissances (Introduction Ă  lâanalyse des sciences, ou de la gĂŠnĂŠration, des fondements et des instruments de nos connaissances).
history: When the $Institut$Institut_de_France$âs moral and political science class debated the connection between signs (in the semiotic sense, where a sign is a symbol embedding the concepts of a culture) and thought, offering a prize for a presentation on the topic "To determine the influence of signs on the formation of ideas," Lancelin submitted an essay that received honorable mention. The winner of the competition was DegĂŠrando (see Ile DegĂŠrando in WA and Cap DegĂŠrando in Tasm). Lancelin later developed his essay and published it between 1801 and 1803 in three volumes under the title Introduction Ă  l'analyse des sciences, ou de la gĂŠnĂŠration, des fondemens, et des instruments de nos connoissances (Introduction to the Analysis of Science, or the Generation, Foundations, and Instruments of Our Knowledge).

characteristic_fr:
LâĂŽle Boullanger est une petite ĂŽle granitique situĂŠe au large de Jurien Bay, sur la cĂ´te de lâAustralie-Occidentale, Ă  environ un kilomĂ¨tre du continent. Elle fait partie de la rĂŠserve naturelle des Fisherman Islands et sâinscrit dans le pĂŠrimĂ¨tre du parc marin de Jurien Bay, un espace protĂŠgĂŠ caractĂŠrisĂŠ par dâimportants herbiers marins, des rivages rocheux, des anses sableuses abritĂŠes et une riche biodiversitĂŠ marine. LâĂŽle revĂŞt une importance ĂŠcologique et biogĂŠographique particuliĂ¨re en tant quâhabitat exclusif du dunnart de lâĂŽle Boullanger (Sminthopsis boullangerensis), un petit marsupial actuellement classĂŠ comme espĂ¨ce vulnĂŠrable. 
La toponymie de lâĂŽle renvoie au contexte des explorations europĂŠennes, puisquâelle a ĂŠtĂŠ nommĂŠe en lâhonneur du gĂŠographe principal de lâexpĂŠdition ayant cartographiĂŠ la rĂŠgion.
characteristic:
Boullanger Island is a small granitic island locatedsouth of Jurien Bay on the coast of Western Australia, approximately one kilometre offshore from the mainland. It forms part of the Fisherman Islands Nature Reserve within the Jurien Bay Marine Park, a protected seascape distinguished by extensive seagrass meadows, rocky shorelines, sheltered sandy bays, and a high diversity of marine life. 
The island is of notable ecological and biogeographical importance as the sole habitat of the Boullanger Island dunnart (*Sminthopsis boullangerensis*), a small marsupial mouse currently classified as a vulnerable species. The island takes its name from the senior geographer associated with the expedition that first charted the area.
history_fr: Depuis la corvette Le GĂŠographe, Boullanger observa la teinte rougeĂ˘tre caractĂŠristique des falaises littorales et dĂŠsigna ce rivage sous le nom de cĂ´te de fer (ÂŤ cĂ´te de fer Âť). Au dĂŠbut du mois de juillet 1801, alors que Le GĂŠographe faisait route vers la baie des Chiens Marins (Shark Bay), il rĂŠalisa un levĂŠ cĂ´tier sous voile de lâĂŽle, consignant ses principaux traits littoraux au fil de la navigation. La persistance du nom de Boullanger dans la toponymie australienne, non seulement avec l'ile Boullanger mais aussi  le cap Boullanger en Australie-Occidentale et la baie Boullanger en Tasmanie, souligne lâhĂŠritage de son Ĺuvre cartographique.
history: From the corvette, Boullanger noted the distinctive reddish hue of the coastal cliffs and designated the shoreline cĂ´te de fer (âiron coastâ). During the early days of July 1801, as Le GĂŠographe sailed northward toward Shark Bay, he conducted a running survey of the island, recording its principal coastal features while underway. The persistence of Boullangerâs name in Australian toponymy is reflected in sites such as Ile Boullanger but also Cap Boullanger in Western Australia and Baie Boullanger in Tasmania, underscoring his chart-making legacy.

characteristic_fr: Aujourdâhui, une partie de ce linĂŠaire cĂ´tier est intĂŠgrĂŠe au parc marin de Jurien, lequel englobe la baie de Jurien et lâĂŽle Boullanger. Cet espace maritime protĂŠgĂŠ abrite dâimportantes colonies de lions de mer australiens, espĂ¨ce rare, ainsi que des phoques, des dauphins, des oiseaux marins, des langoustes australes de lâOuest et une grande diversitĂŠ dâespĂ¨ces de poissons.. La toponymie de la baie de Jurien renvoie Ă  Charles-Marie, vicomte Jurien (1763-1836), alors chef de division au ministĂ¨re de la Marine et des Colonies
characteristic: Today, part of this stretch of coast forms the Jurien Marine Park that encompasses Jurien Bay and Boullanger island and is home to large colonies of rare Australian sea lions, along with seals, dolphins, seabirds, western rock lobsters and a huge variety of fish. Baie Jurien is for Charles-Marie, vicomte Jurien (1763-1836), who was at the time head of division in the ministĂ¨re de la Marine et des Colonies.
history_fr: Jurien, en sa qualitĂŠ dâintermĂŠdiaire capable dâarticuler lâexploration maritime avec la politique et les projets maritimes de la France, constitua un appui essentiel pour Freycinet et supervisa, plus tard, en 1817, les prĂŠparatifs de son voyage de circumnavigation Ă  bord de lâUranie. Charles-Marie Jurien etait le cousin de $Pierre Roch Jurien de La GraviĂ¨re$Pierre_Roch_Jurien_de_La_GraviĂ¨re$ (voir Pointe Jurien en Tasmanie)
history: Jurien, in his capacity to align maritime exploration with French maritime policy and strategic objectives, was an important contact for Freycinet and, later, in 1817, oversaw the preparations for Freycinetâs circumnavigational voyage aboard Uranie. Charles-Marie Jurien was the cousin of $Pierre Roch Jurien de La GraviĂ¨re$Pierre_Roch_Jurien_de_La_GraviĂ¨re$ (see Jurien Point in Tasmania).

characteristic_fr:
Le piton Lesueur, en Australie-Occidentale, est une mesa (montagne tabulaire) remarquable Ă  sommet plat, situĂŠe au sein du parc national de Lesueur et appartenant Ă  la chaĂŽne de Gairdner. SâĂŠlevant abruptement au-dessus dâune ancienne plaine latĂŠritique, sa stratigraphie met en ĂŠvidence une succession de grĂ¨s et de quartzites anciens, surmontĂŠs dâune duricroĂťte rĂŠsistante, une structure qui a permis la conservation de la mesa par ĂŠrosion diffĂŠrentielle tout en engendrant de forts escarpements et des falaises abruptes sur ses marges. Ces conditions gĂŠologiques ont favorisĂŠ la formation dâune mosaĂŻque dâhabitats et contribuĂŠ Ă  des niveaux dâendĂŠmisme particuliĂ¨rement ĂŠlevĂŠs, notamment au sein dâune flore riche en espĂ¨ces de fleurs sauvages, abondantes selon les saisons.
Le toponyme du mont rend hommage Ă  Charles-Alexandre Lesueur (1778â1846), nommĂŠ artiste officiel de lâexpĂŠdition Baudin Ă  la suite de lâescale de celle-ci Ă  lâĂŽle de France (Maurice) en marsâavril 1801 et du dĂŠsistement des artistes Jacques-GĂŠrard Milbert and Michel Garnier initialement dĂŠsignĂŠs par la commission de sĂŠlection des savants.
characteristic:
Mount Lesueur, Western Australia, is a prominent flat-topped mesa (tabletop mountain) located within Lesueur National Park and forming part of the Gairdner Range. Rising abruptly from an ancient lateritic plain, its stratigraphy reveals a sequence of ancient sandstones and quartzites capped by a resistant duricrust, a structure that has preserved the mesa through differential erosion while generating steep escarpments and sheer cliffs along its margins. These geological conditions have fostered a mosaic of habitats and contributed to the areaâs exceptional levels of endemism, particularly among its diverse and seasonally abundant wildflower flora.
The toponym of the mount commemorates Charles-Alexandre Lesueur (1778â1846), who was appointed official artist of the Baudin expedition following its stopover at Ăle de France (Mauritius) in MarchâApril 1801 and the withdrawal of the artists Jacques-GĂŠrard Milbert and Michel Garnier initially designated by the commission responsible for selecting the expeditionâs scientists.
history_fr:
Dans un rapport sur le voyage, prĂŠsentĂŠ au gouvernement par une commission de lâInstitut le 9 juin 1806, son prĂŠsident, Georges Cuvier, mettait en ĂŠvidence lâampleur et la portĂŠe scientifique des collections dĂŠposĂŠes au MusĂŠum par PĂŠron et Lesueur. Il soulignait que celles-ci comprenaient ÂŤ plus de cent mille ĂŠchantillons dâanimaux, parmi lesquels on a dĂŠcouvert plusieurs genres ; que le nombre des espĂ¨ces nouvelles sâĂŠlĂ¨ve Ă  plus de 2 500, et que MM. PĂŠron et Lesueur ont, Ă  eux seuls, fait connaĂŽtre plus dâanimaux que tous les naturalistes voyageurs de ces derniers temps Âť. Cuvier ajoutait que M. Lesueur, qui assistait PĂŠron dans la collecte de ces spĂŠcimens, ĂŠtait en mesure defournir, Ă  travers ses dessins ÂŤ les renseignements les plus exacts sur leur maniĂ¨re de vivre et sur leur habitation Âť.
Au-delĂ  de cette reconnaissance institutionnelle, la relation entre Lesueur et PĂŠron se caractĂŠrisait par une ĂŠtroite collaboration scientifique et une profonde proximitĂŠ personnelle. Compagnons de terrain et partenaires intellectuels, ils menĂ¨rent conjointement leurs travaux dâobservation, de collecte et de description, au point que Lesueur fut parfois perĂ§u, au-delĂ  de son rĂ´le dâartiste, comme un vĂŠritable zoologiste. 
Avant dâassumer les fonctions dâartistes officiels de lâexpĂŠdition aux cĂ´tĂŠs de son collĂ¨gue Nicolas Petit, les deux hommes sâĂŠtaient embarquĂŠs pour le voyage en qualitĂŠ de canonniers auxiliaires. Baudin avait pleinement conscience de leurs talents de dessinateurs et avait prĂŠcisĂŠment favorisĂŠ leur embarquement pour cette raison.
history:
In a report on the voyage presented to the government by a committee of the Institut on 9 June 1806, its chairman, Georges Cuvier, emphasised the scale and scientific significance of the collections deposited at the MusĂŠum by PĂŠron and Lesueur. He noted that they comprised âmore than one hundred thousand animal specimens, among which several new genera were discovered; that the number of new species exceeds 2,500; and that Messrs PĂŠron and Lesueur alone have made known more animals than all the travelling naturalists of recent times.â Cuvier further observed that M. Lesueur, who assisted PĂŠron in collecting these specimens,was able, through his drawings, to provide âthe most accurate information on their modes of life and their habitats.â
Beyond this institutional recognition, the relationship between Lesueur and PĂŠron was characterised by close scientific collaboration and deep personal affinity. Companions in the field as well as intellectual partners, they worked jointly in observation, collection, and description, to such an extent that Lesueur was sometimes regarded, beyond his role as an artist, as a zoologist in his own right. 
Before assuming the positions of the expeditionâs official artists alongside his colleague Nicolas Petit, both men had embarked on the voyage as assistant gunners. Baudin was well aware of their abilities as draftsmen and favoured their embarkation precisely for this reason.

characteristic_fr: Le piton PĂŠron est situĂŠ dans le parc national de Lesueur, en Australie-Occidentale, dans un paysage modelĂŠ par dâanciens milieux marins de dĂŠpĂ´t et de vastes plaines latĂŠritiques. Cet ensemble est dominĂŠ par une sĂŠrie de mesas grĂŠseuses, parmi lesquelles figurent les reliefs que les FranĂ§ais nommĂ¨rent pitons Lesueur et PĂŠron. Ces hauteurs abritent des habitats spĂŠcialisĂŠs qui participent Ă  la biodiversitĂŠ remarquable de la rĂŠgion. Le piton fut nommĂŠ en lâhonneur de FranĂ§ois PĂŠron, zoologiste de lâexpĂŠdition Baudin et principal rĂŠdacteur du rĂŠcit du voyage, dont les travaux demeurĂ¨rent ĂŠtroitement liĂŠs Ă  ceux de son ami et collaborateur Charles-Alexandre Lesueur, auquel fut attribuĂŠ, en ĂŠcho, le nom du relief voisin.
characteristic: The landform named PĂŠron lies within Lesueur National Park in Western Australia, in a landscape shaped by ancient marine depositional environments and extensive lateritic plains. This area is dominated by a series of sandstone mesas, among which are the elevations the French named Lesueur and PĂŠron. These heights support specialised habitats that contribute to the regionâs remarkable biodiversity. The feature was named in honour of FranĂ§ois PĂŠron, zoologist of the Baudin expedition and principal editor of the voyage narrative, whose work remained closely linked to that of his friend and collaborator Charles-Alexandre Lesueur, to whom, in echo, the neighbouring relief was named.
history_fr: PĂŠron ĂŠcrivit que ÂŤ Mon ami M. Lesueur fut toujours mon collaborateur et mon compagnon ; ce que je mâappliquois Ă  dĂŠcrire avec soin, il le dessinoit ou le peignoit lui-mĂŞme avec cette exactitude et cette habiletĂŠ qui lui ont mĂŠritĂŠ tant dâhonorables suffrages Âť. Il sâadressait mĂŞme Ă  Lesueur dans ses lettres en lâappelant ÂŤ Mon cher papa Lesueur Âť, bien quâil eĂťt trois ans de plus que lui, et, Ă  sa mort, il lui lĂŠgua les manuscrits du voyage.
history: PĂŠron states that âMy friend M Lesueur was always my collaborator and companion; what I described with care, he drew or painted it with the accuracy and skill that deserve much praise.â  PĂŠron even addressed Lesueur in letters as âMon cher papa Lesueurâ, despite being three years older, and, at the time of his death, bequeathed to him his manuscripts of the voyage.

characteristic_fr: Sur la carte de lâatlas, la Montagne du Naturaliste est situĂŠe sur la cĂ´te mĂŠdio-occidentale de lâAustralie-Occidentale et sâinscrit dans la partie septentrionale de la rĂŠgion historique connue sous le nom de Terre dâEdels, une dĂŠnomination introduite par les explorateurs nĂŠerlandais en 1619. Lâidentification prĂŠcise de ce relief demeure toutefois incertaine, la frange cĂ´tiĂ¨re ĂŠtant marquĂŠe par la prĂŠsence de nombreuses collines et ĂŠlĂŠvations nommĂŠes, ce qui complique la dĂŠtermination du sommet correspondant Ă  celui dĂŠsignĂŠ par les FranĂ§ais comme Montagne du Naturaliste. Ce toponyme fait rĂŠfĂŠrence au navire Le Naturaliste, commandĂŠ par le capitaine Hamelin.
characteristic: On the atlas chart, Montagne du Naturaliste is located on the mid-west coast of Western Australia and forms part of the northern sector of the historical region known as Edels Land, a toponym introduced by Dutch explorers in 1619. The identification of this feature remains uncertain, however, as the coastal zone is characterised by numerous named hills and elevations, making it difficult to determine precisely which prominence corresponds to the feature designated by the French as Montagne du Naturaliste. The name itself was bestowed in reference to the vessel Le Naturaliste, commanded by Captain Hamelin.
history_fr: Freycinet qualifia ce relief de plateau. Le Naturaliste longea de prĂ¨s la cĂ´te en 1801 alors quâil ĂŠtait Ă  la recherche du GĂŠographe ; cette proximitĂŠ avec le littoral permit Ă  son ĂŠquipage dâidentifier cette forme de relief au cours dâun levĂŠ cĂ´tier en marche.
history: Freycinet described the geographical feature as a plateau. In 1801, the Naturaliste sailed in close proximity to the coast while searching for Le GĂŠographe; this coastal navigation enabled the crew to identify the relief during a running survey conducted from the vessel.

characteristic_fr: La pointe Rouge / Bluff Point se situe Ă  lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ mĂŠridionale de la baie de Gantheaume et doit son nom Ă  la coloration rouge du promontoire, rĂŠsultant de lâoxydation des minĂŠraux ferrugineux contenus dans la roche.
characteristic: Pointe Rouge/Bluff Point lies at the southern end of Gantheaume Bay and owes its name to the red colour of the headland, caused by the oxidation of iron minerals in the rock.
history_fr: Freycinet note: "la pointe Roqge, aprĂ¨s laquelle se dĂŠveloppe la baie Gantheaume, qui eĂťt mĂŠritĂŠ un examen particulier, mais oĂš nous ne sommes point entrĂŠs. VLAMING, qui y jeta lâancre en janvier 1697, dĂŠbarqua sur divers points. Il fait lâĂŠloge de la bontĂŠ de la rade et de lâaspect du pays, quoique aprĂ¨s_plusieurs courses dans lâintĂŠrieur, il nâeĂťt pas ĂŠtĂŠ satisfait de ses productions. Nous avons poussĂŠ un assez grand nombre de bordĂŠes au large de la pointe Rouge, et nous avons trouvĂŠ un bon brassiage depuis 32 jusquâĂ  45 brasses dâeau, selon notre distance de terre."
history: Freycinet notes: "Pointe Rouge, beyond which Gantheaume Bay extends, would have merited closer examination, but we did not enter it. Vlamingh, who anchored there in January 1697, landed at several points along the coast. He praised the quality of the anchorage and the appearance of the country, although, after several excursions inland, he was not satisfied with its resources. We carried out a considerable number of tacks offshore from Pointe Rouge and found good soundings, ranging from 32 to 45 fathoms of water, depending on our distance from the shore."
mapTitle_fr:
Esquisse de la carte de la Terre dâEdels, montrant la route du Naturaliste (an IX) ainsi que celles du GĂŠographe et du Casuarina (an XI), accompagnĂŠes des sondes et des tracĂŠs de relevĂŠs trigonomĂŠtriques. La pointe Rouge y porte initialement le toponyme Victor Hugues, en rĂŠfĂŠrence Ă  lâadministrateur colonial franĂ§ais Victor Hugues, figure politique ambitieuse et controversĂŠe, gouverneur de la Guadeloupe de 1794 Ă  1798 puis de la Guyane de 1799 Ă  1809, MAP/6JJ/5/B, piĂ¨ce 104.
Source : Archives nationales de France.

Pointe Rouge originally bears the toponym Victor Hugues, referring to the French colonial administrator Victor Hugues, an ambitious and controversial political figure who governed Guadeloupe from 1794 to 1798 and French Guiana from 1799 to 1809, MAP/6JJ/5/B, item 104.
Source: National Archives of France.

characteristic_fr: La baie de Gantheaume, Ă  l'embouchure de la riviĂ¨re Murchison, oĂš se trouve la ville de Kalbarri, a ĂŠtĂŠ nommĂŠe en l'honneur de l'amiral franĂ§ais Ganteaume (voir le cap Gantheaume, sur l'ĂŽle Kangourou, et ĂŠgalement la pointe Gantheaume, prĂ¨s de Broome).
characteristic: Gantheaume Bay, at the mouth of the Murchison River, where the town of Kalbarri is located, was named after the French Admiral Ganteaume, (see  Cape Gantheaume, on Kangaroo Island and also Gantheaume Point, near Broome).
history_fr: Hamelin dĂŠcrivit cette baie comme ÂŤ vaste et peu profonde Âť, offrant nĂŠanmoins une protection suffisante grĂ˘ce Ă  la pointe Rouge / Bluff Point pour permettre au Naturaliste de louvoyer et de reprendre sa route (21â22 messidor an IX / 10â11 juillet 1801). Cette apprĂŠciation sâinscrit dans un contexte de navigation active et dâobservation hydrographique, alors que le Naturaliste longeait les cĂ´tes Ă  la recherche du GĂŠographe, dont il avait ĂŠtĂŠ sĂŠparĂŠ Ă  la suite dâune violente tempĂŞte survenue dans la baie du GĂŠographe.
history: Hamelin characterised the bay as âvast and not very deep,â noting that it was sufficiently sheltered by Pointe Rouge/Bluff Point to allow Le Naturaliste to tack effectively and regain its course (21â22 Messidor, Year IX / 10â11 July 1801). His description reflects a practical hydrographic assessment grounded in depth, exposure, and manoeuvrability rather than in scenic qualities alone. These observations were made as Le Naturaliste was conducting a coastal search for Le GĂŠographe, from which it had been separated following a severe storm in Geographe Bay, situating the bay within a broader context of navigation under uncertain conditions and ongoing maritime reconnaissance.

Esquisse de la carte de la Terre dâEdels, montrant la route du Naturaliste (an IX) ainsi que celles du GĂŠographe et du Casuarina (an XI), accompagnĂŠes des sondes et les tracĂŠs de relevĂŠs trigonomĂŠtriques. La baie Gantheaume fut nommĂŠe initialement Baie Hamelin, en l'honneur du capitaine du Naturaliste, MAP/6JJ/5/B, piĂ¨ce 104.
Source : Archives nationales de France.
mapTitle_en:
Sketch map of the Land of Edels, showing the route of the Naturaliste (Year IX) as well as those of the GĂŠographe and the Casuarina (Year XI), together with soundings and some the trigonometric survey lines. Source: National Archives of France.

characteristic_fr: Le cap Bellefin marque lâentrĂŠe occidentale de lâUseless Inlet et doit son nom Ă  JĂŠrĂ´me Bellefin (1764â1835), chirurgien Ă  bord du Naturaliste.
characteristic: Cape Bellefin marks the western entrance to Useless Inlet and takes its name from JĂŠrĂ´me Bellefin (1764â1835), who served as surgeon aboard the Naturaliste during the expedition.
history_fr:
Bellefin participa Ă  la mission dĂŠpĂŞchĂŠe du 25 au 28 juillet 1801, au cours de laquelle un poteau fut ĂŠrigĂŠ sur la cĂ´te orientale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, Ă  proximitĂŠ du cap Bellefin, portant lâinscription suivante : ÂŤ RĂŠpublique franĂ§aise. ExpĂŠdition de dĂŠcouvertes, sous les ordres du capitaine de vaisseau Baudin. La corvette Le Naturaliste ; capitaine Hamelin. 27 messidor an IX [16 juillet 1801]. Âť Voir Cap de l'Inscription, en savoir plus.
Bellefin est ĂŠgalement ĂŠvoquĂŠ pour les moments dâĂŠchange quâil entretint avec les populations aborigĂ¨nes, notamment en partageant des chants tels que La Marseillaise lors des escales en Tasmanie et Ă  Port Jackson, ainsi que pour son rĂ´le, aux cĂ´tĂŠs de PĂŠron, dans la prĂŠservation et lâorganisation des collections zoologiques constituĂŠes au cours du voyage Ă  Port Jackson..
history:
Bellefin joined the party dispatched from July 25 to 28, 1801, during which a pole was erected on the eastern coast of Dirk Hartog Island, next to cape Bellefin, bearing the inscription: âRĂŠpublique franĂ§aise. ExpĂŠdition de dĂŠcouvertes, sous les ordres du Capitaine de Vaisseau Baudin. La corvette le Naturaliste; Capitaine Hamelin. 27 messidor an 9 [16 July 1801].â See Cape Inscription, details
Bellefin was also remembered for sharing songs such as âLa Marseillaiseâ with Aboriginal people, in Tasmania and in Port Jackson, and for looking after the zoological collections with PĂŠron in Port Jackson.
mapTitle_fr:
"Carte de la partie de la baie des Chiens-Marins, sur les cĂ´tes de la Nouvelle-Hollande, dressĂŠe Ă  bord de la corvette Le Naturaliste, par Faure, gĂŠographe". Le cap Bellefin est originellement nommĂŠ Pointe DĂŠsirĂŠe, MAP/6JJ/4/A, piĂ¨ce 58.
Source : Archives nationales de France.

"Chart of part of Shark Bay on the coasts of New Holland, drawn aboard the corvette Le Naturaliste, by Faure, geographer." Cape Bellefin was originally named Pointe DĂŠsirĂŠe, MAP/6JJ/4/A, item 58.
Source: National Archives of France.

characteristic_fr: La forme et la situation du Mondrain de la Direction en font un repĂ¨re de navigation clairement identifiĂŠ par son toponyme.
characteristic: La forme et la situation du Mondrain de la Direction en font un repĂ¨re de navigation clairement identifiĂŠ par son nom.
history_fr: Freycinet indique que, pour emprunter le chenal ĂŠtroit qui sĂŠpare la pointe mĂŠridionale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog du continent et donne accĂ¨s Ă  la baie Shark, les navigateurs doivent longer de prĂ¨s la cĂ´te Ă  hauteur de la pointe EscarpĂŠe (Steep Point), puis mettre le cap sur une faible ĂŠminence culminant Ă  environ 50 mĂ¨tres. Ce repĂ¨re fut dâabord dĂŠsignĂŠ sur les premiers croquis sous le nom de Pilon de la Direction, avant dâĂŞtre rebaptisĂŠ de maniĂ¨re plus gĂŠographique Mondrain de la Direction (Mount Direction).
history: Freycinet notes that, to enter the narrow channel separating the southern tip of Dirk Hartog Island from the mainland and providing access to Shark Bay, navigators should keep close to the shore at Pointe EscarpĂŠe (Steep Point), then steer toward a low hill rising to about 50 metres. This feature was first labelled on early sketches as Pilon de la Direction (âDirection Pillarâ) and was later given the more topographically descriptive name Mondrain de la Direction (Mount Direction).

characteristic_fr:
Useless Inlet est un ĂŠtroit bras de mer qui s'ĂŠtend vers l'intĂŠrieur des terres et qui est reliĂŠ Ă  Denham Sound.
Faisant partie de la zone classĂŠe au patrimoine mondial de Shark Bay en Australie, il est aujourd'hui connu pour lâimportante production de sel exploitĂŠe Ă  titre privĂŠ Ă  Useless Loop.
Son nom reflĂ¨te le fait que les navires ne peuvent pas naviguer dans ce petit bras de mer, le rendant dĂŠpourvu de toute utilitĂŠ pratique pour la navigation et le transport maritime.
characteristic:
Useless Inlet is a narrow waterway extending inland and connected to Denham Sound. 
Part of Australia's Shark Bay World Heritage Area, it is now known for its large, privately-run salt production at Useless Loop.
Its name reflects the fact that vessels would not be able to navigate into this small inlet, rendering it of no practical use for shipping.
history_fr: Freycinet rapporte avoir dĂŠcouvert  un petit havre "LâextrĂŠmitĂŠ MĂŠridionale se termine par un bassin fort agrĂŠable,", malheureusement obstruĂŠ par un banc de sable (aujourdâhui dĂŠsignĂŠ sous le nom de outer bar) et dont la profondeur nâĂŠtait que de 97 cm. Les navires ne pouvant y accĂŠder, il le nomma Havre Inutile, ou Useless Inlet.
history: Freycinet reports that he discovered a small harbour, âwhose southern extremity ends in a very pleasant basin,â unfortunately obstructed by a sandbar (now known as the outer bar) and with a depth of only 97 cm. As vessels were unable to enter it, he named it Havre Inutile, or Useless Inlet.

characteristic_fr: Le cap Heirisson marque lâentrĂŠe orientale de Useless Inlet. Ce toponyme rend hommage aux travaux menĂŠs par FranĂ§ois Antoine Boniface Heirisson, officier aux cĂ´tĂŠs de Freycinet Ă  bord du Le Naturaliste, sur et autour de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog. Il participa Ă  une mission de reconnaissance menĂŠe du 21 au 27 juillet 1801, au cours de laquelle des vents dĂŠfavorables perturbĂ¨rent Ă  plusieurs reprises lâitinĂŠraire prĂŠvu.
characteristic: Cape Heirisson forms the eastern entrance to Useless Inlet. The toponym commemorates the work of Freycinetâs fellow officer aboard Le Naturaliste, FranĂ§ois Antoine Boniface Heirisson, carried out on and around Dirk Hartog Island. He participated in a surveying mission between 21 and 27 July 1801, during which adverse winds repeatedly disrupted the planned route.
history_fr: Heirisson se montrait trĂ¨s critique Ă  lâĂŠgard de Hamelin, dont il disait quâil ÂŤ calomnie toujours tout le monde, tandis quâen privĂŠ il leur tĂŠmoigne des marques dâamitiĂŠ Âť. AprĂ¨s son retour de mission dans la baie Shark, Heirisson exprima sa frustration envers son commandant en dĂŠchirant devant lui son propre croquis de la Swan River, convaincu que Hamelin avait mis en doute ses capacitĂŠs en son absence. Freycinet fut toutefois redevable Ă  Heirisson de lâesquisse sommaire couvrant le secteur du cap Levillain* au Coin de Mire*, qui lui permit dâachever sa propre carte de la Shark Bay, ainsi que de ses travaux antĂŠrieurs sur la Swan River, lesquels contribuĂ¨rent Ă  lâĂŠlaboration de la carte de lâEdels Land. (voir Ăle Heirisson, WA, pour plus de dĂŠtails)
history: Heirisson was critical of Hamelin, of whom he said that he âalways slanders everybody while, in private, he shows them sign of friendships.â After his return from his mission in Shark Bay, Heirisson expressed his frustration with his commander by tearing up his own sketch of the Swan River in front of him, believing that Hamelin had expressed some scepticism about his ability behind his back. However, Freycinet was indebted to Heirisson for his rough chart from cap Levillain* to le Coin de Mire*, which allowed him to complete his own chart of Shark Bay, and for Heirissonâs earlier work at the Swan River, which contributed to the preparation of the chart of Edels Land. (see Ăle Heirisson, WA, for details)

Le toponyme cap Heirisson, en rouge, a ĂŠtĂŠ ajoutĂŠ ultĂŠrieurement au DĂŠpĂ´t des cartes, Ă  Paris, MAP/6JJ/5/B, piĂ¨ce 106.
Source : Archives nationales de France.

characteristic_fr: Le cap Ransonnet est le point le plus mĂŠridional de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog. Il est inclus dans les limites de la zone du patrimoine mondial de la baie de Shark Bay depuis 1991. Il doit son nom Ă  Jacques Joseph Ransonnet (1778-1861), membre de lâĂŠquipage de lâexpĂŠdition Baudin, aspirant de deuxiĂ¨me puis de premiĂ¨re classe, Ă  bord du Naturaliste en 1801 (Voir aussi Baie Ransonnet en Tasmania).
characteristic: Cape Ransonnet  is the southernmost point of Dirk Hartog Island, inscribed within the boundaries of  the World Heritage Area of Shark Bay since 1991. It was named after Jacques Joseph Ransonnet (1778-1861) was a crew member of the Baudin expedition, midshipman 2nd class, then 1st class, on board the Naturaliste in 1801 (See also Baie Ransonnet in Tasmania).
history_fr: Le 18 juillet 1801, Hamelin envoya une embarcation lĂŠgĂ¨re, placĂŠe sous les ordres de Ransonnet, afin de sonder la profondeur des eaux dans la baie situĂŠe au sud du mouillage du *Naturaliste* [baie de Denham]. Ransonnet y nota des âanomalies remarquablesâ du rĂŠgime des marĂŠes autour de lâĂŽle Dirck-Hartighs [Hartog].
history: On the 18 July 1801, Hamelin sent the small boat under the orders of Ransonnet  to sound the depth of the waters in the bay to the south of le Naturalisteâs anchorage [Dehnam Sound]. Ransonnet recorded âstriking anomaliesâ of the tides, around the Dirck-Hartighs [Hartog]  island.

characteristic_fr: Le Coin de Mire se situe sur la cĂ´te orientale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, qui constitue la limite occidentale de la baie de Shark Bay. Cette formation calcaire en pente douce sâavance dans la baie et, en tant que pointe mĂŠridionale de Herald Bay, constitue un repĂ¨re de navigation remarquable, offrant de vastes vues sur les approches de lâĂŽle Egg, comme le suggĂ¨re son toponyme.
characteristic: Quoin Bluff lies on the eastern coast of Dirk Hartog Island, which marks the western boundary of Shark Bay. This gently sloping limestone formation projects into the bay and, as the southern headland of Herald Bay, serves as a prominent navigational landmark, offering wide-ranging views over the approaches to Egg Island, as its toponym suggests.
history_fr: Freycinet rapporte, Ă  propos de sa mission de deux semaines, du 2 au 14 aoĂťt 1801, que "dans la partie de la baie des Chiens-Marins, qui est entre Dirck-Hartighs et la presquâĂŽle PĂŠron,  un grand nombre de bancs de sable rĂŠpandus dans cet espace ont pour limites, dâune part, le cap Lesueur, et, de lâautre, le Coin-de-Mire". Il prĂŠcise que "la navigation est libre au N. de la ligne qui joindroit ces deux points; dans le S., elle exige de grandes prĂŠcautions et ne convient quâĂ  de petits navires, souvent mĂŞme quâaux plus lĂŠgĂ¨res embarcations."
history: Freycinet reports on his two-week mission, from 2 to 14 August 1801, that, âin the part of the Bay of Seals lying between Dirck-Hartighs and the Peron Peninsula, a large number of sandbanks scattered across this area are bounded, on the one hand, by Cape Lesueur and, on the other, by Coin-de-Mire.â He further specifies that ânavigation is unobstructed to the north of the line joining these two points; to the south, it requires great caution and is suitable only for small vessels, and often only for the lightest boats.â
mapTitle_fr: Ăbauche cartographique parmi les plus anciennes de la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay de Dampier), Terre dâEndracht, dressĂŠe par Louis de Freycinet lors de la mission de juillet 1801. Elle fait apparaĂŽtre le Coin de Mire, dont le toponyme a ĂŠtĂŠ conservĂŠ dans la nomenclature, contrairement Ă  la majoritĂŠ des autres noms proposĂŠs Ă  cette date. MAP/6JJ/4/A, piĂ¨ce 59.
mapTitle_en: One of the earliest cartographic sketches of the Bay of Chiens-Marins (Dampierâs Shark Bay), on the Land of Eendracht, drawn by Louis de Freycinet during the July 1801 mission. It shows Coin de Mire, a toponym that was retained in the nomenclature, unlike the majority of the other names proposed at that time. MAP/6JJ/4/A, item 59.

characteristic_fr: Tetradon Loop se situe sur la cĂ´te orientale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, au sein de la zone du patrimoine mondial de la baie de Shark Bay, en Australie-Occidentale. Ce lieu doit son nom Ă  lâabondance de tĂŠtrodons observĂŠe par les FranĂ§ais dans les eaux de la baie.
characteristic: Tetradon Loop is located on the east coast of Dirk Hartog Island, within the Shark Bay World Heritage Area in Western Australia. It takes its name from the abundance of tĂŠtrodons observed by the French in the waters of the bay.
history_fr: Au cours de sa mission de deux semaines, du 2 au 14 aoĂťt 1801, Freycinet visita une petite baie et un ĂŽlot oĂš lui-mĂŞme et ses marins dĂŠcouvrirent, le 3 aoĂťt 1801, de nombreux tĂŠtrodons (poissons-globes), dâoĂš les appellations baie des TĂŠtrodons et ĂŽle des TĂŠtrodons.
history: During his two-week mission, from 2 to 14 August 1801, Freycinet visited a small bay and an islet where he and his sailors found many tĂŠtrodons (globefish) on 3 August 1801, hence the names Baie des TĂŠtrodons and Ăle des TĂŠtrodons.

characteristic_fr: Egg Island se situe au large de la cĂ´te orientale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog et sâest fait connaĂŽtre dans les annĂŠes 1950 pour lâexploitation du guano. Les FranĂ§ais lâavaient initialement nommĂŠe ĂŽle du TĂŠtrodon (voir Tetradon Loop).
characteristic: Egg Island lies off the east coast of Dirk Hartog Island and became known in the 1950s for guano mining. The French initially named it Tetrodon Island (see Tetradon Loop).
history_fr: Au cours de sa mission de deux semaines, du 2 au 14 aoĂťt 1801, Freycinet visita une petite baie et un ĂŽlot oĂš lui-mĂŞme et ses marins dĂŠcouvrirent, le 3 aoĂťt 1801, de nombreux tĂŠtrodons (poissons-globes), dâoĂš les appellations baie des TĂŠtrodons et ĂŽle des TĂŠtrodons.
history: During his two-week mission, from 2 to 14 August 1801, Freycinet visited a small bay and an islet where he and his sailors found many tĂŠtrodons (globefish) on 3 August 1801, hence the names Baie des TĂŠtrodons and Ăle des TĂŠtrodons.
mapTitle_fr: bauche cartographique parmi les plus anciennes de la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay de Dampier), Terre dâEndracht, dressĂŠe par Louis de Freycinet lors de la mission de juillet 1801. Elle fait apparaĂŽtre les sondes bathymĂŠtriques, la route de la chaloupe, ainsi que lâĂŽlot des TĂŠtrodons. MAP/6JJ/4/A, piĂ¨ce 60.

It shows the bathymetric soundings, the route taken by the boat, as well as âĂŽlot des TĂŠtrodons/Egg Island. MAP/6JJ/4/A, item 60.

characteristic_fr: La pointe Notch, aujourdâhui intĂŠgrĂŠe au parc national de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, offre un abri naturel Ă  la baie attenante, caractĂŠristique gĂŠographique qui explique lâappellation qui lui fut donnĂŠe par lâexpĂŠdition franĂ§aise : pointe du Refuge.
characteristic: Notch Point, now incorporated within Dirk Hartog Island National Park, affords natural shelter to the adjoining bay, a geographical characteristic that underlies the name bestowed upon it by the French expedition: Pointe du Refuge.
history_fr: Au cours de sa mission de deux semaines, du 2 au 14 aoĂťt 1801, Freycinet trouva "refuge" dans "une crique bien fermĂŠe et commode" Âť, adossĂŠe Ă  une pointe qu'il nomma Pointe du Refuge.
history: During his two-week mission, from 2 to 14 August 1801, Freycinet sought shelter in âa well enclosed and convenient coveâ adjacent to Point Refuge.
mapTitle_fr: Ăbauche cartographique parmi les plus anciennes de la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay de Dampier), Terre dâEndracht, dressĂŠe par Louis de Freycinet lors de la mission de juillet 1801. Elle fait apparaĂŽtre les sondes bathymĂŠtriques, la route de la chaloupe, ainsi que la pointe du Refuge et la crique attenante, dont le tracĂŠ fut ultĂŠrieurement rectifiĂŠ Ă  Paris en rouge. MAP/6JJ/4/A, piĂ¨ce 60.

It shows the bathymetric soundings, the route taken by the boat, as well as Pointe du Refuge/Notch Point and the adjoining cove, the outline of which was later rectified in red in Paris. MAP/6JJ/4/A, item 60.

characteristic_fr: Le cap Levillain est un promontoire sablonneux et isolĂŠ situĂŠ Ă  lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ nord-orientale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog. Il tire son nom de Stanislas Benjamin Levillain (1774â1801, ĂŠgalement orthographiĂŠ Le Villain), qui exerĂ§a les fonctions de zoologiste Ă  bord du Naturaliste.
characteristic: Cape Levillain is a remote sandy promontory on the north-eastern tip of Dirk Hartog Island. It takes its name from Stanislas Benjamin Levillain (1774â1801, also spelled Le Villain), who served as the zoologist aboard Le Naturaliste.
history_fr:
Levillain demanda Ă  se joindre Ă  la mission chargĂŠe de hisser un pavillon Ă  lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ septentrionale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, dây dĂŠposer une bouteille contenant un message informatif de Hamelin attestant du passage du Naturaliste dans ces eaux, et dâobserver les marĂŠes. Il se trouve que cette mission mit au jour le poteau de Vlaminck portant sa double inscription (voir cap de lâInscription).
De son cĂ´tĂŠ, il recueillit et naturalisa par la suite des spĂŠcimens dâoiseaux â notamment des pĂŠtrels, des cormorans, des damiers et des huĂŽtriers â destinĂŠs au MusĂŠum national dâHistoire naturelle de Paris.
history: Levillain requested to join the mission that was instructed to raise a flag on the northern extremity of Dirk Hartog Island, to deposit a bottle containing an informative message from Hamelin recording the passage of Le Naturaliste through these waters, and to observe the tides. As it happens, this mission encountered Vlaminckâs pole bearing its dual inscription (see Cape Inscription). For his part, he collected and later prepared taxidermy specimens of birdsâincluding petrels, cormorants, damiers, and oystercatchersâdestined for the MusĂŠum national dâHistoire naturelle in Paris.

Source : National Library of Australia

characteristic_fr:
Ă lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ la plus septentrionale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog, le cap de lâInscription constitue le site des plus anciennes prises de terre europĂŠennes connues sur la cĂ´te occidentale du continent australien. Le premier dĂŠbarquement europĂŠen attestĂŠ sur la cĂ´te ouest de lâAustralie fut effectuĂŠ le 25 octobre 1616 par Dirk Hartog, capitaine du navire Eendracht de la Compagnie nĂŠerlandaise des Indes orientales. Ă cette occasion, Hartog laissa une plaque dâĂŠtain, gravĂŠe dâun rĂŠcit de sa visite, quâil cloua sur un poteau dressĂŠ verticalement dans une fissure rocheuse au sommet de la falaise.
Cette plaque fut rĂŠcupĂŠrĂŠe en 1697 par Willem de Vlamingh lors de son voyage de dĂŠcouverte, qui la remplaĂ§a par une autre plaque dâĂŠtain portant lâinscription de son propre passage. Cette seconde plaque ĂŠtait encore en place lorsquâelle fut retrouvĂŠe en juillet 1801 par Le Thiers, quartier-maĂŽtre et maĂŽtre dâĂŠquipage de lâexpĂŠdition de Hamelin, envoyĂŠ en mission sur lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog les 20 et 21 juillet 1801. Hamelin choisit de laisser la plaque de Vlamingh en place et y ajouta sa propre inscription sur une feuille de plomb, clouĂŠe Ă  un autre poteau.
Câest en rĂŠfĂŠrence Ă  cette  succession dâinscriptions que lâexpĂŠdition franĂ§aise dĂŠsigna ultĂŠrieurement le site sous le nom de cap de lâInscription.
characteristic:
At the most northerly point of Dirk Hartog Island,Cape Inscription is the site of the earliest known landings of Europeans on the western coast of the Australian continent. The first known European landing on the west coast of Australia was by Dirk Hartog of the Dutch East India Company's ship the Eendracht at Cape Inscription on 25 October 1616.  Hartog left a pewter plate, inscribed with a record of his visit and nailed to a post left standing upright in a rock cleft on top of the cliff. Dirk Hartog's plate was recovered by Willem de Vlamingh during his voyage of discovery in 1697.  He in turn left a pewter plate inscribed with a record of his visit.  The plate left by Vlamingh was in turn found in 1801 by Hamelin's quartermaster/chief yeoman, Le Thiers,that Hamelin sent in mission in Dirk Hartog island on the 20-21 July 1801 Hemelin left the plate in place and added his own inscription on a piece of lead sheet, nailed to another post.
It was in reference to this succession of inscriptions that the French expedition subsequently designated the site Cap de lâInscription.
history_fr:
voir dans "Les rapports de mission illustrĂŠs", Cap de l'Inscription.
Sur le premier plan levĂŠ par Faure Ă  bord du Naturaliste, le toponyme envisagĂŠ ĂŠtait ÂŤ pointe du Retour Âť, afin de marquer le fait que, lors de leur mission de reconnaissance de la Baie des Chiens Marins, Freycinet et Faure atteignirent ce point septentrional avant de retourner vers la corvette. Toutefois, Hamelin opta pour une dĂŠsignation dâune portĂŠe plus historique et mĂŠmorielle en donnant au cap un nom faisant ĂŠcho Ă  la succession des commandants qui, au fil des voyages, y avaient inscrit la trace de leur passage au moyen dâinscriptions gravĂŠes sur des plaques dressĂŠes au sommet du cap.
history:
see in "Illustrated mission reports", Cap Inscription.
On the earliest chart drawn by Faure aboard the Naturaliste, the toponym initially proposed was âPointe du Retourâ (âReturn Pointâ), intended to mark the fact that, during their reconnaissance of the Bay of Chiens-Marins, Freycinet and Faure reached this northernmost point before turning back toward the corvette. Hamelin, however, opted for a designation of broader historical and memorial significance, bestowing upon the cape a name that alluded to the succession of commanders who, over the course of successive voyages, had inscribed the trace of their passage there by means of engraved inscriptions on plaques erected at the summit of the cape.

characteristic_fr:
Les deux principales entrĂŠes de la baie des Requins portent le nom des deux corvettes de lâexpĂŠdition, le GĂŠographe et le Naturaliste.
Le passage du Naturaliste donne accĂ¨s Ă  la partie mĂŠridionale des ĂŽles-barriĂ¨res de Bernier et de Dorre.
characteristic:
The two main entrances to Shark Bay bear the names of the expeditionâs two corvettes, le GĂŠographe and le Naturaliste.
The Passage du Naturaliste provides access to the south of the barrier islands of Bernier and Dorre.
history_fr:
Le GĂŠographe jeta lâancre dans la baie des Chiens Marins [Shark Bay] le 28 juin 1801, soit plus de deux semaines avant lâarrivĂŠe du Naturaliste. Baudin ne patienta toutefois pas suffisamment pour permettre Ă  son bĂ˘timent consort de le rejoindre : le GĂŠographe quitta la baie des Requins le 14 juillet, tandis que le Naturaliste mouilla Ă  lâest de lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ septentrionale de lâĂŽle Dirk Hartog le 16 juillet. Les deux navires ne se retrouvĂ¨rent que le 21 septembre, Ă  Timor.
Dans lâintervalle, les deux gĂŠographes de lâexpĂŠdition â Boullanger Ă  bord du GĂŠographe et Faure Ă  bord du Naturaliste â furent envoyĂŠs en missions distinctes de reconnaissance et entreprirent le levĂŠ systĂŠmatique de la baie.
history:
The GĂŠographe anchored in Shark Bay on 28 June 1801, more than two weeks before the arrival of the Naturaliste. Baudin, however, did not wait long enough for his consort to rejoin him:The GĂŠographe departed Shark Bay on 14 July, while the Naturaliste dropped anchor to the east of the northern tip of Dirk Hartog Island on 16 July. The two vessels were reunited only on 21 September at Timor.
In the meantime, the expeditionâs two geographersâBoullanger aboard the GĂŠographe and Faure aboard the Naturalisteâwere dispatched on separate reconnaissance missions and began the systematic charting of the bay.*

characteristic_fr: Le cap mĂŠridional de lâĂŽle Dorre fut nommĂŠ en lâhonneur de Jacques de Saint-Cricq (1781â1819), sous-lieutenant Ă  bord du Naturaliste.
characteristic: The southern cape of Dorre Island was named after Jacques de Saint-Cricq (1781-1819), Sub-lieutenant on board le Naturaliste.
history_fr: Le 18 juillet 1801, Hamelin envoya Saint-Cricq dans la partie mĂŠridionale de lâĂŽle Dorre. Il lui fut demandĂŠ de vĂŠrifier la prĂŠsence ĂŠventuelle de rĂŠcifs, de repĂŠrer des plages propices au dĂŠbarquement, de procĂŠder Ă  des observations sur les marĂŠes et sur lâhistoire naturelle du secteur, de dresser un pavillon franĂ§ais et de dĂŠposer sur la plage une bouteille contenant un message attestant du passage du Naturaliste, ainsi que de rechercher dâĂŠventuelles traces dâun dĂŠbarquement antĂŠrieur sur lâĂŽle par des membres de lâĂŠquipage du GĂŠographe. Saint-Cricq fut frappĂŠ par le caractĂ¨re rocheux de lâĂŽle, quâil dĂŠcrivit comme suit: "Je suis le seul des officiers du bord, qui ai visitĂŠ lâIsle de Dorre, et, encore, n'ai je ĂŞtĂŠ qu'Ă  la pointe du SO ; ce que j'ai vu m'a fait prĂŠsumer que cette ĂŽsle n'est qu'un rocher que les siĂ¨cles ont agrandi, peu Ă  peu ; en effet, comment ne pas avoir cette opinion d'une masse ĂŠnorme de rocs entassĂŠs les uns sur les autres par couches trĂ¨s rĂŠguliĂŠres ? Couches, dont la hauteur et le nombre diminuent graduellement jusqu'aux bords de la mer, oĂš, Ă  ving."
history: On 18 July 1801, Hamelin sent Saint-Cricq to the southern part of Dorre Island. He was asked  to check for the presence of reefs, for suitable beaches to land, to make observations on tides and on the areaâs natural history, to erect a French flag and leave a bottle on the beach containing a note to attest to the visit of le Naturaliste, and to check for signs of an earlier landing on the island by crew members of le GĂŠographe. Saint Cricq was impressed by the rock-strewn nature of the island that he described as âan enormous mass of rocks piled up one on top of the other in very regular layersâ. In recognition of his work, the southern cape of the island was named after him. (See also Baie de lâAttaque).

characteristic_fr:
SituĂŠe Ă  lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ nord-occidentale de la zone inscrite au patrimoine mondial de la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay), presque exactement Ă  lâouest de Carnarvon, en Australie-Occidentale, lâĂŽle Bernier constitue un habitat dâune grande valeur biologique. Elle abrite lâune des rares populations encore existantes de wallabies-liĂ¨vres Ă  bandes, ainsi quâune espĂ¨ce de rongeur aujourdâhui menacĂŠe, le djoongari, ĂŠgalement connu sous le nom de souris de Shark Bay.
LâĂŽle a ĂŠtĂŠ nommĂŠe en lâhonneur de lâastronome de lâexpĂŠdition, Pierre-FranĂ§ois Bernier (1779-1803).
LâĂŽle doit son nom Ă  lâastronome de lâexpĂŠdition, Pierre-FranĂ§ois Bernier (1779-1803).
characteristic:
Situated at the north-western extremity of the Shark Bay World Heritage area, almost directly west of Carnarvon in Western Australia, Bernier Island constitutes a habitat of considerable biological value. It supports one of the few remaining populations of the banded hare-wallaby, as well as a now endangered rodent species, the djoongari, also known as the Shark Bay mouse.
The island was named in honour of the expeditionâs astronomer, Pierre-FranĂ§ois Bernier (1779â1803).
It was named after the astronomer of the expedition, Pierre-FranĂ§ois Bernier (1779-1803).
history_fr: Le 8 juillet 1801, en compagnie de Louis Depuch et de Nicolas Baudin, Bernier se rendit au nord-est de lâĂŽle Bernier ÂŤ afin dâen dĂŠterminer la latitude et la longitude et de fixer avec prĂŠcision lâentrĂŠe de la baie des Chiens-MarinsâŚ Âť. Pour relever les angles et les distances nĂŠcessaires au levĂŠ de cette portion du littoral de lâĂŽle, lâobservatoire du GĂŠographe dut y ĂŞtre installĂŠ (voir la carte ci-contre). Voir ĂŠgalement le cap Bernier en Tasmanie.
history: On 8 July 1801, accompanied by Louis Depuch and Nicolas Baudin, Pierre-FranĂ§ois Bernier proceeded to the north-eastern part of Bernier Island âin order to determine its latitude and longitude and to fix precisely the entrance to Shark BayâŚâ. To take the angles and distances required for charting this section of the islandâs coastline, the observatory of the GĂŠographe had to be established there (see the accompanying chart). See also Cape Bernier inTasmania.

characteristic_fr: Le cap Ronsard constitue lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ septentrionale de lâĂŽle Bernier. Il a ĂŠtĂŠ nommĂŠ en lâhonneur de FranĂ§ois-Michel Ronsard (1769â1836), ingĂŠnieur de la Marine Ă  bord du GĂŠographe, ultĂŠrieurement promu au grade de lieutenant.
characteristic:
Cape Ronsard forms the northernmost headland of Bernier Island. It was named after FranĂ§ois-Michel Ronsard (1769â1836), a naval engineer aboard Le GĂŠographe, who was later promoted to the rank of lieutenant.
named after FranĂ§ois-Michel Ronsard (1769-1836), a marine engineer on board le GĂŠographe who was promoted to lieutenant.
history_fr: Dans son journal, il dĂŠcrivit le sol sablo-calcaire de lâĂŽle comme ÂŤ peu favorable Ă  la vĂŠgĂŠtation Âť, mais ĂŠvoqua une mer bordant ses rivages abondant dâune ÂŤ quantitĂŠ prodigieuse de poissons Âť. Au cours de ses excursions, Ronsard sâattacha Ă  repĂŠrer des sources de bois susceptibles de servir Ă  la rĂŠparation ou Ă  la reconstruction des embarcations du navire, tĂ˘che dont il avait la responsabilitĂŠ. Baudin apprĂŠciait ses compĂŠtences, tout comme le ministre de la Marine Pierre-Alexandre-Laurent Forfait (ministre de la Marine avant la nomination de Denis DecrĂ¨s), qui aurait probablement soutenu sa candidature Ă  lâexpĂŠdition. Les relations entre Ronsard et son collĂ¨gue officier Henri Freycinet furent en revanche conflictuelles, en raison dâun diffĂŠrend portant sur lâanciennetĂŠ. La rĂŠsolution de cette question revĂŞtait une importance particuliĂ¨re pour les deux officiers dans lâĂŠventualitĂŠ du dĂŠcĂ¨s de Baudin.
history: In his journal, he described the islandâs sandy limestone soil as ânot very suitable for vegetationâ but wrote of the sea along its shores as full of a âprodigious quantity of fishesâ. On his excursions, Ronsard kept a look-out for sources of wood to repair or reconstruct the vesselâs boats, for which he had responsibility. Baudin valued his skills, as did the Navy Minister Pierre-Alexandre-Laurent Forfait (the Minister of the Navy before Denis DecrĂ¨s' appointment) who probably fostered his candidacy for the voyage. The relationship between Ronsard and his fellow officer Henri Freycinet were rancorous, due to a dispute over seniority. The resolution of this would be of particular importance to both officers in the event of the death of Baudin.

characteristic_fr: Le Passage du GĂŠographe permet lâaccĂ¨s Ă  la baie situĂŠe au nord des ĂŽles-barriĂ¨res de Bernier et de Dorre. Il tire son nom du navire amiral de lâexpĂŠdition.
characteristic: Geographe Channel provides access to the bay north of the barrier islands of Bernier and Dorre. It is named after the expeditionâs flagship.
history_fr: The GĂŠographe passed through the channel on 27â28 June 1801 and then dropped anchor to the south of it, off Bernier Island (for information on the corvette, see Geographe Bay).
history: Le GĂŠographe franchit le passage les 27 et 28 juin 1801, puis jeta lâancre au sud de celui-ci, Ă  la hauteur de lâĂŽle Bernier (pour des informations sur la corvette, voir baie du GĂŠographe).

characteristic_fr:
SituĂŠ Ă  l'ouest de la pĂŠninsule de Peron, le havre Henri Freycinet, ĂŠgalement connu sous le nom d'estuaire Freycinet, est l'un des golfes intĂŠrieurs de Shark Bay. Le port se caractĂŠrise par une densitĂŠ d'ĂŽles plus ĂŠlevĂŠe que celle du havre Hamelin, situĂŠ Ă  proximitĂŠ, et, dans sa partie nord, par une sĂŠrie de pĂŠninsules ĂŠtroites appelĂŠes localement ÂŤ prongs Âť.
IntĂŠgrĂŠ au parc marin de Shark Bay et inclus dans la zone du patrimoine mondial de Shark Bay, l'estuaire a ĂŠtĂŠ identifiĂŠ comme un habitat important pour les dugongs et fait partie d'un environnement marin plus vaste rĂŠputĂŠ pour sa spĂŠcificitĂŠ ĂŠcologique, notamment la prĂŠsence de stromatolites. ParallĂ¨lement, le port se trouve dans une zone qui a subi des modifications industrielles, notamment par le dragage associĂŠ Ă  la Shark Bay Salt Joint Venture.
Le havre a ĂŠtĂŠ nommĂŠ par Louis de Freycinet en l'honneur de son frĂ¨re aĂŽnĂŠ &Henri&Louis Henri_de_Saulces_de_Freycinet& (voir aussi Presqu'ĂŽle Freycinet, Tasmanie).
characteristic:
Situated to the west of the Peron Peninsula, Henri Freycinet Harbour, also known as Freycinet Estuary, is one of the inner gulfs of Shark Bay. The harbour is characterised by a higher density of islands than the neighbouring Hamelin Pool and, in its northern sector, by a series of narrow peninsulas locally known as 'prongs'.
Incorporated within the Shark Bay Marine Park and included in the Shark Bay World Heritage Area, the estuary has been identified as an important habitat for dugongs and forms part of a broader marine environment noted for its ecological specificity, including the presence of stromatolites. At the same time, the harbour lies within a zone that has undergone industrial modification, notably through dredging associated with the Shark Bay Salt Joint Venture.
The harbour was named by Louis de Freycinet in honour of his elder brother Henri (see also PresquâĂŽle Freycinet, Tasmania).
history_fr:
Freycinet rapporte que des bancs de sable semblent obstruer presque entiĂ¨rement lâentrĂŠe du havre, dont lâindentation sâĂŠtend selon un axe sud-estânord-ouest. Il en ĂŠvalue la longueur Ă  vingt-deux lieues (environ 120 km) et la largeur Ă  deux ou trois lieues (un peu plus de 15 km). Bien que le havre ne soit pas entiĂ¨rement exempt de bancs, il souligne que son bassin central comprend nĂŠanmoins de nombreux secteurs de grande profondeur, tandis que des hauts-fonds se succĂ¨dent sans interruption le long des marges. La navigation y est donc souvent possible dans les eaux centrales plus profondes, mais demeure pĂŠrilleuse Ă  proximitĂŠ des rivages. Freycinet mentionne ĂŠgalement la prĂŠsence dâĂŠtangs salĂŠs le long des rivages, au nord de la presquâĂŽle PĂŠron. 
PĂŠron, en revanche, sâattarde longuement sur la prĂŠsence des dugongs dans ses descriptions du havre Henri Freycinet et du havre Hamelin. Pour expliquer lâappellation erronĂŠe de ÂŤ Shark Bay Âť donnĂŠe par Dampier, il observe que le navigateur anglais nâavait sous les yeux quâune tĂŞte dĂŠjĂ  partiellement dĂŠcomposĂŠe par la "digestion". Il ajoute que les deux dents allongĂŠes mentionnĂŠes par Dampier â autre source de confusion â se rencontrent Ă  la fois chez lâhippopotame et chez le dugong, ne diffĂŠrant que par leur position : portĂŠes Ă  la mĂ˘choire infĂŠrieure chez le premier, et Ă  la mĂ˘choire supĂŠrieure chez le second, PĂŠron, tome 2, p.229.
history:
Freycinet reports that sandbanks appear almost entirely to obstruct the entrance to the harbour, whose indentation extends along a south-eastânorth-west axis. He estimates its length at twenty-two leagues (approximately 120 km) and its width at two to three leagues (just over 15 km). Although the harbour is not entirely free of banks, he notes that its central basin nevertheless includes numerous areas of considerable depth, while shoals occur continuously along the margins. Navigation is therefore often possible in the deeper central waters, but remains hazardous close to the shores. Freycinet also notes the presence of salt ponds along the shoreline north of the Peron Peninsula.
PĂŠron, by contrast, dwells at length on the presence of dugongs in his descriptions of both Henri Freycinet Harbour and Hamelin Harbour. In explaining the erroneous name âShark Bayâ given by Dampier, he observes that the English navigator had only a head partially decomposed by digestion before him. He further notes that the two elongated teeth mentioned by Dampierâan additional source of confusionâare found in both the hippopotamus and the dugong, differing only in their position: in the former they are borne by the lower jaw, in the latter by the upper.

LâĂŽle Lefebvre est une petite ĂŽle situĂŠe au sein du havre Henri Freycinet, dans la partie mĂŠridionale de la zone inscrite au patrimoine mondial de Shark Bay.
Elle porte le nom de Jean Thomas Lefebvre, quartier-maĂŽtre sur le Naturaliste, puis sur Le GĂŠographe.
characteristic:
Čle Lefebvre is a small island situated within the Henri Freycinet Harbour, in the southern portion of the Shark Bay World Heritage area. 
It is named  after Jean Thomas Lefebvre, quartermaster on le Naturaliste, and then Le GĂŠographe,
history_fr: Freycinet dĂŠcrivit lâĂŽlot comme ÂŤ trĂ¨s escarpĂŠ et dâun abord facile Âť et qualifia Lefebvre de ÂŤ mon patron de canot et excellent timonier Âť.
history: Freycinet described the islet as "very steep and easy of access" and referred to Lefebvre,as âmy skipper of the boat and excellent helmsmanâ.

characteristic_fr:
Depuch Loop est une ouverture donnant accĂ¨s Ă  un petit bras de mer, depuis laquelle on peut aujourdâhui apercevoir le monticule blanc de sel des exploitations saliniĂ¨res dâUseless Loop, situĂŠ Ă  quelque 250 kilomĂ¨tres.
Freycinet lui donna le nom dâEntrĂŠe Depuch, ÂŤ en lâhonneur de lâun de nos compagnons les plus estimables et les plus malheureux Âť, le gĂŠologue principal de lâexpĂŠdition.
characteristic:
Depuch Loop is an opening into a small inlet, from which one can today see the white salt mound of the salt works at Useless Loop, some 250 kilometres away.
Freycinet gave it the name EntrĂŠe Depuch, âafter one of our worthiest and most unfortunate companionsâ, the expeditionâs senior geologist.
history_fr: PĂŠron travailla de concert avec Depuch Ă  la conception dâun appareil destinĂŠ Ă  mesurer la tempĂŠrature de la mer Ă  de grandes profondeurs. Depuch mourut de dysenterie en fĂŠvrier 1803. PĂŠron note Ă  son sujet : ÂŤ M. Depuch, rĂŠduit au dernier degrĂŠ de marasme par la dysenterie. Ce jeune et infortunĂŠ savant, aussi prĂŠcieux par sa modestie et la bontĂŠ de son cĹur que par lâĂŠtendue et la variĂŠtĂŠ de ses connaissances, ne devait jamais revoir sa terre natale ! Âť.voir ĂŠgalement lâanse Depuch, en Australie-Occidentale.
history: FranĂ§ois PĂŠron worked closely with Louis Depuch on the design of an instrument intended to measure sea temperature at great depths. Depuch died of dysentery in February 1803. PĂŠron wrote of him: âM. Depuch, reduced to the final stage of wasting by dysentery. This young and unfortunate savant, as remarkable for his modesty and the kindness of his heart as for the breadth and variety of his knowledge, was never to see his native land again.â  see also Anse Depuch, Western Australia

characteristic_fr: Ce cap bas, sableux et aride, situĂŠ Ă  lâentrĂŠe Est de lâanse Depuch, reĂ§ut le nom de pointe Giraud en lâhonneur dâĂtienne Stanislas Giraud, aspirant de premiĂ¨re classe Ă  bord du Naturaliste (voir ĂŠgalement le Rocher Giraud, en Australie-Occidentale).
characteristic: This low, sandy, and arid headland at the eastern entrance to Depuch Loop was named Point Giraud after Ătienne Stanislas Giraud, Midshipman First Class aboard the Naturaliste (see also Rocher Giraud, Western Australia).
history_fr:
Lors de sa mission, Freycinet, espĂŠrant trouver une source dâeau douce, constate :ÂŤ LâentrĂŠe Depuch, dont la pointe Giraud est lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ nord-est, se dessine comme lâembouchure dâune riviĂ¨re ; mais le faible courant quâon y remarque et la sĂŠcheresse complĂ¨te du sol nous portent Ă  penser quâelle nâest quâun embranchement de la mer. Plusieurs petits ĂŽlots, en nombre assez considĂŠrable, se trouvent Ă  son entrĂŠe ; tous prĂŠsentent une stĂŠrilitĂŠ complĂ¨te.
La pointe Giraud, basse, aride et fort aiguĂŤ, se termine par une langue de sable qui sâĂŠtend sous lâeau et va se confondre avec les bancs. Âť
history:
During his mission, Freycinet, hoping to find a source of fresh water, observed:
âDepuch Inlet, of which Point Giraud forms the north-eastern extremity, presents the appearance of a river mouth; however, the weak current perceptible there and the complete aridity of the surrounding ground lead us to believe that it is merely an arm of the sea. Several small islets, fairly numerous, lie at its entrance; all exhibit complete sterility.
Point Giraud, low, arid, and very sharp in outline, terminates in a sandy tongue that extends beneath the water and merges with the surrounding banks.â

characteristic_fr:
Located in the southern part of Henri Freycinet Harbour, and measuring only about one kilometre in width by two kilometres in length, Salutation Island is today known as the habitat of the vulnerable greater stick-nest rat (Leporillus conditor, VU).
Freycinet named the island after the botanist Jean-Baptiste Leschenault de la Tour (see also Port Leschenault), but observed that âLeschenault Island âŚ is completely barren: a multitude of seabirds appear to have made their home there.â
characteristic:
Located in the south of Henri Freycinet harbour, measuring only about one kilometre wide by two kilometres long, Salutation Island is now the habitat of the vulnerable  species of the greater stick-nest rat Leporillus conditor (VU).
Freycinet named the island after the botanist Leschenault (see the entry Port Leschenault), but notes " Leschenault Island ... is completely barren: a multitude of seabirds appear to have made their home there."
history_fr:
Tout en prenant part aux pratiques de collecte de spĂŠcimens propres Ă  lâhistoire naturelle de son temps, lesquelles impliquaient notamment la mise Ă  mort dâanimaux Ă  des fins dâĂŠtude, Leschenault se montrait nĂŠanmoins sensible au caractĂ¨re dur et implacable de lâabattage menĂŠ par les chasseurs de lâexpĂŠdition, qui tuaient des kangourous dans la baie des Chiens-Marins â des animaux qui, notait-il,  nâont dâautres ennemis Ă  redouter que quelques aigles de mer, mais sous des buissons impĂŠnĂŠtrables, ils peuvent braver la voracitĂŠ de ces oiseaux, nous vinmes troubler leur retraite, et jetter au milieu deux la terreur, on en tua beaucoup."
Il rapporte en outre que "un de ceux-ci moins prompt que les autres Ă  sortir de sa retraite fut saisi ; ce petit animal ne parĂťt nullement effayĂŠ, il lĂŠcha mĂŞme celui qui venait dâĂŞtre lâassasin de sa mĂ¨re et prit la nourriture qui lui fut prĂŠsentĂŠe, le commandant la conservĂŠ pendant plusieurs mois, et il perit accidentellement Ă  Timor, il sâetoit parfaitement apprivoisĂŠ, etoit caressant, et facile Ă  nourrir, il mangeoit du pain et aimoit beaucoup lâeau sucrĂŠe.
history:
While participating in the practices of specimen collection typical of natural history at the time, including the killing of animals for study, Leschenault was nonetheless sensitised to the harsh and relentless slaughter carried out by the expeditionâs hunters, who killed kangaroos in Shark Bayâanimals that, as he observed, âhave no enemies to fear other than a few sea eagles; by taking refuge beneath impenetrable bushes, they defy the voracity of these birds. We came and brought strife and terror to their refugeâŚâ.
He further recounts that âone of the young kangaroos, which was slower than the others in leaving its place of shelter, was captured; and instead of appearing frightened, it licked the man who had just killed its mother and accepted the food offered to it. The Commander kept the animal for several months, until it died accidentally at Timor. It became perfectly tame, affectionate, and easy to feed. It ate bread and was particularly fond of sugar water.â

characteristic_fr: Three Bays Island dĂŠsigne une ĂŽle situĂŠe dans la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay), en Australie-Occidentale, ainsi nommĂŠe en raison de ses trois baies distinctes. Elle fait partie intĂŠgrante du Shark Bay World Heritage Area, inscrit au patrimoine mondial de lâUNESCO. Freycinet, qui lui donna ce nom, prĂŠcise Ă  son sujet que ÂŤ lâĂŽle aux Trois-Baies a pris son nom de sa forme Âť.
characteristic: Three Bays Island refers to an island located in Shark Bay, Western Australia, so named for its three distinct bays. It forms an integral part of the Shark Bay World Heritage Area, inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List. Freycinet, who bestowed the name, noted that âthe Island of Three Bays took its name from its shape.â
history_fr: Freycinet, qui en fit la reconnaissance en aoĂťt 1801, prĂŠcise quâÂŤ elle est passablement boisĂŠe et dâune hauteur moyenne. Lâabordage y est commode, mais le brassiage trĂ¨s faible nâen permet lâapproche quâaux canots Âť.
history: Freycinet, who surveyed it in August 1801, noted that âit is moderately wooded and of average height. Landing there is easy, but the very shallow depth allows approach only by small boats.â

characteristic_fr:
Lâisthme Taillefer relie la Peron Peninsula/Presqu'Čle PĂŠron au continent australien, dans la rĂŠgion de Gascoyne (baie des Chiens-Marins / Shark Bay).
$Hubert Taillefer$Hubert _Jules_Taillefer$ (1779â1866) fut le second chirurgien Ă  bord du GĂŠographe, puis, Ă  partir de novembre 1801, Ă  bord du Naturaliste.
characteristic:
Taillefer Isthmus  connects Peron Peninsula with the coast of Western Australia in the Gascoyne region (Shark bay). 
Hubert Taillefer (1779-1866) was the second surgeon on board Le GĂŠographe, and from November 1801, on le Naturaliste.
history_fr:
Louis de Freycinet rendit hommage aux trois chirurgiens de lâexpĂŠdition en leur attribuant des toponymes dans la baie des Chiens-Marins (Shark Bay) : la baie Lharidon, lâisthme Taillefer et le cap Bellefin.
Lors de lâĂŠlaboration de la planche de lâatlas, Freycinet confronta ses propres esquisses cartographiques Ă  celles rĂŠalisĂŠes par Pierre Faure, gĂŠographe Ă  bord du Naturaliste. Il dĂŠcrit ainsi sa mĂŠthode de travail cartographique : "Nâayant entre les mains que le plan dressĂŠ par M. FAURE et non ses observations; ne connoissant dâailleurs par aucune donnĂŠe certaine la largeur de lâisthme Taillefer, jâai ĂŠtĂŠ rĂŠduit Ă  la supposer, et jâai conservĂŠ Ă  mes constructions lâĂŠtendue que je leur avois donnĂŠe. Ce point une fois fixĂŠ, jây ai assujetti le plan de M. FAURE, câestâĂ âdire que je lâai fait pivoter autour de la pointe des Hauts-Fonds, jusquâĂ  ce que lâisthme Taillefer eĂťt acquis la largeur convenue."
history:
Louis de Freycinet honoured the three surgeons of the expedition by assigning their names to geographical features in Shark Bay: Baie Lharidon, Isthme Taillefer, and Cap Bellefin.
When preparing the atlas plate, Freycinet compared his own sketches with those produced by Pierre Faure, the geographer aboard the Naturaliste. He explains his cartographic method as follows:
âHaving only the plan drawn up by Mr FAURE, and not his observational notes, and lacking any reliable data on the width of the Taillefer Isthmus, I was obliged to make an assumption and to retain the dimensions I had adopted in my constructions. Once this point had been established, I adjusted Mr FAUREâs plan accordingly; that is to say, I rotated it about the tip of the Hauts-Fonds until the Taillefer Isthmus acquired the agreed width.â

characteristic_fr: Pointe Moreaau/Eagle Bluff se caractĂŠrise par une haute falaise dominant le Denham Sound et se situe Ă  environ 18 Ă  20 kilomĂ¨tres au sud de Denham. Le site fut nommĂŠ par les FranĂ§ais en lâhonneur de lâaspirant Charles Moreau (voir ĂŠgalement lâentrĂŠe Moreau, Australie-Occidentale).
characteristic: Eagle Bluff is characterised by a prominent cliff overlooking Denham Sound and lies approximately 18 to 20 kilometres south of Denham. The site was named by the French in honour of the midshipman Charles Moreau (see also the entry Moreau, Western Australia).
history_fr: Le capitaine Emmanuel Hamelin envoya Ă  terre le 6 aoĂťt 1801, avec neuf hommes sous ses ordres, sur la PĂŠron Peninsula. Leur mission consistait Ă  dresser des tentes, Ă  rĂŠparer la chaloupe et Ă  procĂŠder Ă  la coupe de bois destinĂŠ au navire.L'aspirant Moreau revint a bord du Naturaliste le 11 aout 1801
history: Captain Emmanuel Hamelin dispatched on 6 August 1801, with nine men under his command, to go ashore on the Peron Peninsula. Their mission was to pitch tents, carry out repairs to the expeditionâs rowing boat, and cut wood for the ship.Moreau returned aboard the Naturaliste on 11 August 1801.

characteristic_fr:
La pĂŠninsule PĂŠron constitue un ĂŠlĂŠment structurant de la baie des Chiens-Marins, inscrite au patrimoine mondial, au sein de la rĂŠgion de la Gascoyne. Lâensemble de la baie se caractĂŠrise par une configuration gĂŠnĂŠrale en forme de ÂŤ W Âť, la pĂŠninsule PĂŠron formant une ligne de partage entre le Hamelin Pool et le havre Henri Freycinet.
Les EuropĂŠens ont d'abord considĂŠrĂŠ cette terre comme une ĂŽle distincte, dĂŠsignĂŠe sous le nom de Middle Island, une interprĂŠtation liĂŠe Ă  une connaissance encore imparfaite de la gĂŠographie intĂŠrieure de la baie. Ă lâissue de la reconnaissance menĂŠe par lâexpĂŠdition Baudin, la pĂŠninsule reĂ§ut le nom de FranĂ§ois PĂŠron, principal savant de lâexpĂŠdition (voir ĂŠgalement le canal PĂŠron, en Tasmanie).
characteristic:
The Peron Peninsula constitutes a structuring element of Shark Bay, a World Heritageâlisted area within the Gascoyne region. As a whole, the bay displays a characteristic âWâ-shaped configuration, with the PĂŠron Peninsula forming a line of division between Hamelin Pool and Henri Freycinet Harbour.
Europeans initially regarded this land as a distinct island, known as Middle Island, an interpretation reflecting their still imperfect understanding of the bayâs internal geography. Following the reconnaissance conducted by the Baudin expedition, the peninsula was named after FranĂ§ois PĂŠron, the expeditionâs principal savant (see also Canal PĂŠron, in Tasmania).
history_fr:
Le 19 mars 1803, PĂŠron accompagna une chaloupe envoyĂŠe Ă  terre pour explorer le secteur oriental de la presquâĂŽle. Agissant de sa propre initiative et sans coordination prĂŠalable, il entreprit une excursion le long de la cĂ´te, oĂš il recueillit des ĂŠchantillons de sel dans des ĂŠtangs qui furent par la suite dĂŠsignĂŠs sous le nom dâĂŠtangs Montbazin. Au cours de cette reconnaissance, un coup de feu tirĂŠ sur un requin amena PĂŠron Ă  se replier vers lâintĂŠrieur des terres ; il se dissimula alors avec ses compagnons aprĂ¨s avoir aperĂ§u des AborigĂ¨nes descendant des dunes voisines, rĂŠagissant face au son de l'arme Ă  feu et Ă  la prĂŠsence inattendue du groupe. Aucun contact nâeut lieu.
Ătant alourdis par les spĂŠcimens collectĂŠs et manquant dâeau, le retour vers la chaloupe se rĂŠvĂŠla pĂŠnible et retarda la rĂŠintĂŠgration Ă  bord du GĂŠographe, ce qui suscita lâirritation de Baudin.
history:
On 19 March 1803, PĂŠron accompanied a longboat party sent ashore to explore the eastern sector of the peninsula. Acting on his own initiative and without prior coordination, he undertook a further excursion along the coast, collecting salt samples from lakes later known as the ĂŠtangs Montbazin. During this foray, a gunshot fired at a shark prompted PĂŠron to withdraw inland, where he concealed himself with his companions after observing Aboriginal people moving down from nearby dunes and reacting  to the the sound of the firearm and the unexpected presence of the party. No contact followed.
Being burdened by the specimens they had collected and lacking water, the return to the longboat proved arduous and delayed their re-embarkation aboard the GĂŠographe, thereby provoking Baudinâs irritation.

characteristic_fr:
Le cap Lesueur constitue lâextrĂŠmitĂŠ occidentale de la pĂŠninsule PĂŠron. Ce promontoire marque la limite nord-orientale du havre Henri Freycinet et correspond Ă  une inflexion nette du tracĂŠ cĂ´tier, qui sâoriente ensuite vers le nord-est.
Le cap fut nommĂŠ en lâhonneur de Alexandre Lesueur, lâun des artistes de lâexpĂŠdition et proche ami de PĂŠron (voir ĂŠgalement Piton Lesueur, en Australie-Occidentale).
characteristic:
Cape Lesueur marks the westernmost extremity of the Peron Peninsula. The headland defines the north-eastern limit of Henri Freycinet Harbour and coincides with a distinct change in the orientation of the coastline, which thereafter trends towards the north-east.
The cape was named in honour of Alexandre Lesueur, one of the expeditionâs artists and a close companion of PĂŠron (see also Piton Lesueur, Western Australia).
